bioIntro: Carole Ward is a missionary, leader, and teacher fostering an indigenous missions movement in Northern Uganda and South Sudan. She moved to Uganda in 2002 during a 20-year horrific war, where she served and trained African leaders from all backgrounds to pray.

Joy Daniels was born in Quebec, Canada. Raised in a missionary's home, Joy witnessed people's lives transformed through the many revivals her father conducted. Joy attended Zion Bible Institute which helped and guided her in her walk with the Lord.

Stephanie Maier is a seasoned leader with more than 25 years of experience advancing democratic institutions and empowering communities across 14 countries in conflict and post-conflict regions. From Afghanistan and Tajikistan to Cambodia, Haiti, the Middle East...

Terry-Lynne Goodman has devoted more than 30 years to global missions, preaching and teaching in over 25 countries through conferences, Bible colleges, churches, and leadership forums. Raised in a missionary-pastor’s home in Canada, she embraced her faith early and sensed a call to...

Jared is a widely-learned connector and vision builder who serves leaders and organizations needed in the world today. An aspiring polymath who has constantly sought the whole truth, he has given his service to Favor since 2012 to support this unique model of national transformation

After serving in the U.S. Marine Corps, Mike Blickensderfer attended and graduated from Ohio University in 1974 receiving a degree in business administration. He subsequently attended Seton Hall University School of Law, receiving his law degree in 1985.

Alex has over 25 years of financial and executive experience providing finance, accounting, and strategic leadership for growth-oriented private, non-profit organizations, and non-governmental organizations. Alex – who has lived and worked in Asia, Latin America, and Africa

For 37 years, David worked at Fort Knox as an operations research analyst, conducting cost effectiveness studies of potential new military equipment, and as an “urban planner,” monitoring design and construction of construction projects on Fort Knox.

Martha Mott has been an international conference speaker and Bible teacher for over 28 years. As a licensed and ordained minister, she has served on various ministry boards and compensation committees. In 2012, Martha founded Hearts Desire International Ministries,

Lisa Coggin was born again into the Kingdom of God through her belief and acceptance of Jesus as our Savior and now lives every day in unswerving allegiance to Him. Lisa and her husband Keith have been married for 42 years and have served the Lord in Uganda and East Africa for 17 years.

Dr. Louis Kayatin and his wife Tina have pastored and grown Church on the North Coast for over 40 years. He is an accomplished author, leadership consultant, and missionary of prayer. His decades of work building intercessory networks in Ohio and Uganda
A native of Peru, Dr. Vega is an international speaker and co-founder of a non-profit that sends doctors and nurses on mission trips to Africa, Asia, and Latin America.
